records.prompt: For the '2026 FIFA World Cup Winner' event, rank Spain, France, England, South Korea, New Zealand, and Switzerland by implied probability, 24h volume, bid-ask spread, and exit quality, then show the results as a chart plus a tradability table.
records.operatorNotes: Response rendered Event Rankings + Market Tradability Data tables only; no canvas/chart heading; page hit scroll edge with no chart visible after search/scroll scan.

records.prompt: For 'Will Bitcoin hit $150k by June 30, 2026?', chart the bid-ask spread, orderbook depth, and simulated slippage for $1k, $5k, and $10k YES positions, with a tradability recommendation.
records.operatorNotes: Response renders 1 slippage table + Market Liquidity bullet list + Tradability prose; assistant text claims 'provided visualizations' but no canvas/chart actually drawn; page at scroll edge.

records.prompt: Chart how live trading attention is distributed across 'Will Bitcoin hit $150k by June 30, 2026?', 'Will the Iranian regime fall by May 31?', 'US x Iran permanent peace deal by May 31, 2026?', 'Will the U.S. invade Iran before 2027?', and 'Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by May 15?' using 24h volume, current probability, open interest, and spread.
records.operatorNotes: Agent explicitly stated 'An inline chart was not rendered for this turn'; response is 1 comparison table + Key Insights bullets; OI/24h-vol data also reported missing.

records.prompt: For 'Eurovision Winner 2026', rank France, Estonia, Austria, Albania, Belgium, Croatia, Czechia, and Germany by whale exposure, current probability, and 24h volume, then chart which outcomes have meaningful smart-money support.
records.operatorNotes: Plotted chart visible: 8 outcomes on x-axis, dual y-axes (whale exposure $, probability/support 0-1), 4-series legend (Probability, Smart-money support, Volume24h, Whale exposure). Found via browser_search("smart-money") + 1 scroll-down step.

records.prompt: For '2026 FIFA World Cup Winner', chart live volume share and implied probability for Spain, France, England, South Korea, New Zealand, Switzerland, and Haiti, then identify which outcomes have volume that looks outsized relative to probability.
records.operatorNotes: Plotted chart visible: title "2026 FIFA World Cup Winner: Live volume share and implied probability", dual y-axes (Volume 0-38M, Price 0-0.18), x-axis End Date, 2-series legend (Price, volume), blue declining line + green spiky volume series. Found via browser_search("volume share") match 2 of 2.
